items.summary: Lebanese state media has reported Israeli strikes, some deadly, at about 30 locations in the country's south despite a partial ceasefire.

items.title: UN warns countries to prepare for moderate to strong El Niño amid deadly heatwave in India

items.title: Melbourne Renegades BBL licence to be sold as Cricket Victoria establishes new team
items.summary: The Melbourne Renegades BBL licence is set to be sold to a private investor, with Cricket Victoria to establish a new team, marking the end of the Stars as well.

items.title: Coroner questions parents on use of mobile phone, early morning laundry during infant death inquest
items.summary: A coronial inquest is being held into the death of 18-week-old, premature-born twins, who cannot be identified for legal reasons, who died in their Far North Queensland home in late 2021.

items.title: One Nation SA says it will not support Labor's bills as it seeks more parliamentary resources
items.summary: South Australia's One Nation has vowed to block every Labor government bill following a dispute over staffing allocations.

items.title: Katter attempts to force discussion on abortion access in Queensland parliament despite ban on debate
items.summary: The subject became an issue ahead of the last Queensland election in October 2024, with Premier David Crisafulli implementing a ban on abortion debate after winning government.

items.title: Prominent obesity surgeon Dr Vahid Reza Adib suspended from practising medicine
items.summary: Obesity surgeon Reza Adib has been suspended from practising medicine as he faces rape charges.

items.title: Inquest considers whether care of Adelaide man's bed sores was 'appropriate and reasonable'
items.summary: A coronial inquest will investigate whether an aged care facility in Adelaide's inner north appropriately treated the bed sores a 79-year-old man with dementia developed in the lead-up to his death.

items.title: Beer industry shocked by closure of Tasmanian James Boag's brewery

items.title: Search continues for missing Tasmanian devil at popular Gold Coast wildlife park
items.summary: The recently arrived young devil has been kept in a secure indoor facility while quarantining, but was noticed missing Tuesday morning.

items.title: Mitchell Moses's Eels absence raises concerns over potential Origin return

items.title: Expensive watches, work bags and gold-plated stationery claimed in SA Health scheme
items.summary: The Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) has called for stronger governance of professional development reimbursements for public sector medical officers, with some staff making claims for luxury items like watches and work bags.

items.title: Under Notre Dame, a 'dig of the century' looks to unearth thousands of years of Parisian history
items.summary: More than a year after France's famed Notre Dame reopened, an archaeological exploration going thousands of years into the history of Paris has begun just metres below the cathedral's ground floor.

items.title: Almost one-third of NT children in care allegedly harmed in 2024-25, report finds

items.summary: NSW Police will move to the mandatory use of body-worn cameras for sworn officers in the wake of allegations of a culture of impunity within the police force.

items.title: ABARES report predicts broadacre farm profits will drop by 70 per cent
items.summary: The ABARES report for the June quarter predicts national crop production will fall by more than 20 per cent, with profits down 70 per cent for broadacre farms.

items.title: Steady is a joy, but raising a dog wasn't what I'd imagined

items.title: Fair Work Commission annual ruling lifts minimum wage by 6 per cent and award rate by 4.75 per cent
items.summary: Australia's minimum wage will increase by 5.97 per cent, and minimum award workers will get a 4.75 per cent pay boost, in the Fair Work Commission's (FWC) annual wage review.

items.title: Race to develop Ebola vaccine heats up amid deadly outbreak in DRC

items.summary: A series of upsets across Roland Garros leaves world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the box seat to win the claycourt grand slam after beating Naomi Osaka.

items.summary: Millions of workers will get a 4.75 per cent pay rise after Fair Work raised modern award wages above the rate of inflation. Catch up on the day's financial news and insights from our specialist business reporters on our blog.

items.title: Farmers flock to buy CCTV cameras to stop fuel theft

items.title: Emirates proposes increase in helicopter flights at luxury Wolgan Valley resort

items.title: Voters consider alternatives to major political parties as One Nation popularity surges

items.title: Birds return to Little Dog Island in Bass Strait after feral cats eradicated

items.summary: Carly Sheppard is perhaps best known for experimental performance, but a coincidental meeting with a fellow dancer convinced her to go back to her roots.

items.title: UK releases more than 1,000 pages of documents relating to Peter Mandelson appointment
items.summary: Among the more than 1,500 pages of documents were messages from Peter Mandelson voicing strong criticisms of Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer's leadership of the UK government.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: 'Awestruck' Grenvold bolts into the Diamonds squad
items.summary: Despite signing her first full-time Super Netball contract this year there was no guarantee Jessie Grenvold would get a lot of court time. But through injury, the Mavericks defender took on a starting spot and is now an Australian Diamond.

items.title: Identity of man at centre of alleged extramarital relationship extortion remains suppressed
items.summary: The name of a high-profile Queenslander entwined in an extortion charge must temporarily remain under wraps while police prepare an application to prevent it from becoming public knowledge.

items.summary: It was a "boys' trip to the races" that went wrong, a Supreme Court jury in Toowoomba has heard.
